mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.76% as of 2007-10-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts from prior peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2007-10-01, near cyclical lows but vulnerable to deterioration
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE rising > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and housing starts jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously by more than 5% over a 2-month window, it indicates a demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1191k units (2007-09-01) shows housing market stress; RSAFS at 380.3B reflects consumer spending pressure
regime_hint: demand contraction phase following credit tightening
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not precede negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This constrains earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.357 (2007-09-01) reflects manufacturing sector stress amid credit crisis
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 50,000 persons per month, indicating labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138,421k (2007-10-01) shows employment near peak but vulnerable to credit shock transmission
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS MoM growth < 50k are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or signal triggers in < 15% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling demand weakness that reduces equity earnings forecasts. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,630.7B (2007-07-01 vintage) reflects Q2 2007 growth before credit crisis deepened
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% AND inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed is in restrictive tightening mode. This dual condition signals monetary constraint that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.76% and CPIAUCSL at 208.903 (2007-10-01) reflect tightening bias amid inflation concerns
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint condition does not precede negative 20-day returns in > 58% of instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1,000k units annualized while retail sales simultaneously contract, indicating a demand shock originating in the housing sector that spreads to consumer spending. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as credit conditions tighten.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1000k annualized AND RSAFS declines > 2% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1191k (2007-09-01) approaching trigger threshold; RSAFS at 380.3B reflects consumer spending stress from housing downturn
regime_hint: housing-led demand contraction with credit spillover
failure_condition: Joint condition does not precede negative 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction coefficient magnitude is smaller than individual HOUST or RSAFS coefficients in multivariate model.
